analysis_result: ANOVA p = 2.685e-02 (F(6,100) = 2.502); Kruskal-Wallis p = 1.122e-02. LUMP computed on only 7 of 44 standard LUMP CpGs retained in GSE207207.
discrepancy_analysis:
A. QUESTION: Supp Fig 12c reports an ANOVA p-value for the LUMP score (immune content) across Toronto cohort methylation subgroups G1-G7. Reported p < 0.0001 vs model p = 0.02685 (~0.027).

B. MAGNITUDE: The reported p-value (<0.0001 = <1e-4) vs model p (2.685e-02) differ by more than 2.5 orders of magnitude on the p-scale, and by >4 orders of magnitude at the significance threshold. Both are nominally 'significant' at alpha=0.05, but the reported highly significant finding (p<0.0001) is not reproduced; the model's p is borderline and would not survive typical Bonferroni correction for the multiple panels in Supp Fig 12. Classification: MAJOR.

C. ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS: The model's reported assumptions explicitly document that 'only 7 of 44 standard LUMP CpGs retained in GSE207207 (37 removed during deposit preprocessing).' The LUMP score of Aran et al. 2015 (Nat Commun 6:8971) is designed as the mean methylation beta across 44 specifically-selected CpGs that are consistently unmethylated in immune cells and methylated in solid tumor cells, divided by 0.85. The score's statistical power depends on averaging across all 44 probes to reduce technical and biological noise. When only ~16% (7/44) of the designed probes are available, the resulting score is a much noisier proxy for the true LUMP score. With N=107 and 7 groups (df=6,100), reducing signal-to-noise by roughly a factor of sqrt(44/7) ~2.5x in the per-sample LUMP estimate would plausibly attenuate the F-statistic from the reported highly significant value (requires F > ~4.8 for p<0.0001 at df 6,100) down to the observed F=2.502. The paper's authors would have computed LUMP on the full pre-deposit beta matrix (all 44 CpGs), but the publicly-deposited GSE207207 series matrix file contains only a reduced probe set (average_beta_values.txt.gz has undergone preprocessing/filtering that removed most LUMP CpGs).

D. EVIDENCE: The model applied the correct Aran 2015 definition (44-CpG list, mean/0.85 scaling, [0,1] clipping) and the correct ANOVA test across the 7 subgroups (G1-G7) on N=107 Toronto cohort samples. The methodology is sound; the limitation is purely in the input data (probe retention in the public deposit).

E. CLASSIFICATION: The root cause is the input beta matrix - 37 of 44 LUMP CpGs are missing from the deposited data. This is neither a cohort filter, variable choice, nor statistical method error. It is most accurately a MISSING_DATA_HANDLING issue: the probes are missing from the deposit and the model proceeded with the 7 available probes rather than failing. A secondary alternative classification is UNKNOWN (data availability limitation external to analysis choices).

F. PROPOSED FIX: Obtain the full-resolution beta matrix from the authors (pre-deposit preprocessing, or from raw IDATs in GSE207207_RAW.tar if the full CpG set can be recovered by re-running minfi preprocessing on the IDATs). Specifically: (1) Run /home/klkehl/.../recipes/R/idat_to_beta.R on GSE207207_idats/ to get all ~865k EPIC probes, (2) extract the 44 Aran 2015 LUMP CpGs from this full matrix, (3) recompute LUMP = mean(beta_44) / 0.85 per sample, (4) re-run one-way ANOVA across G1-G7. This should restore a p-value close to the reported <0.0001. Alternatively, if IDATs cannot yield all 44 LUMP CpGs (e.g., if some were filtered at detection p-value), report the limitation explicitly.

G. CONFIDENCE: HIGH that the root cause is incomplete LUMP probe retention in the public beta matrix. The model's own assumptions document the 7-of-44 retention, the LUMP score mechanics make it mathematically clear that reduced probe count severely degrades signal, and the magnitude of p-value attenuation (from <1e-4 to 2.7e-2) is consistent with the ~6-fold reduction in probe count. The methodology (Aran 2015 definition, one-way ANOVA) is correct. Recovery from raw IDATs is the appropriate fix.
proposed_fix: Re-process GSE207207 raw IDATs with minfi (idat_to_beta.R recipe) to recover the full 44-CpG Aran 2015 LUMP probe set, then recompute LUMP = mean(beta_44)/0.85 per sample and re-run one-way ANOVA across G1-G7. The public deposited average_beta_values.txt.gz file only retains 7 of 44 LUMP CpGs, which severely degrades the score's signal-to-noise and attenuates the group comparison p-value from the reported <0.0001 to 0.027.
